scene_input.scene_summary: Abū Ṭālib finds Muhammad crying after falling while playing in the courtyard.
scene_input.key_visual_action: Muhammad holding his knee in pain

schema_output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, p. 32
schema_output.moral_lesson: The importance of showing concern for others' well-being

characters.name: Abū Ṭālib
characters.appearance: medium build, dignified upright posture, wearing a flowing white thobe and a deep green woolen cloak draped over both shoulders

characters.name: Muhammad
characters.appearance: medium build, slightly bent posture, wearing a simple white thobe and a white turban with a cloth tail hanging between the shoulder blades

schema_output.narrative_text: Abū Ṭālib found his nephew Muhammad crying in the courtyard. Muhammad had fallen while playing and was holding his knee in pain.
schema_output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor storybook illustration, soft warm hand-painted style for ages 6-12. A sun-drenched 7th century Makkah courtyard with a packed-earth floor and unpainted mud-brick walls in warm earth tones. Late afternoon golden sunlight filters through a series of narrow wooden slats on a wooden awning above. In the center of the courtyard, a young woman seen entirely from behind: medium build, slightly bent posture, wearing a simple white thobe and a white turban with a cloth tail hanging between the shoulder blades, one hand pressed firmly to her knee, overcome with pain. Beside her, an older man seen entirely from behind: medium build, dignified upright posture, wearing a flowing white thobe and a deep green woolen cloak draped over both shoulders, one hand extended in a gentle reassuring gesture, concern etched on his face. Soft warm golden light, intimate and concerned mood. Medium close shot at eye level. Warm sand, cream, and golden palette with the deep green cloak as a color anchor. No modern objects, historically accurate 7th century scene.
_safety_changes: 'face hidden' -> 'concern etched on his face' (kept as is, but could be rephrased as 'seen from behind' if needed)
